title: Diplotaxis setosa
taxonomy: Diplotaxis setosa is a species of plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification places Diplotaxis setosa within the kingdom Plantae and phylum Magnoliophyta. The family Brassicaceae comprises a wide range of species, including broccoli, cauliflower, and mustard plants. Diplotaxis setosa is further classified as a member of the genus Diplotaxis, which contains several species of wild or garden mustard. The binomial nomenclature of Diplotaxis setosa was established by botanists to provide a unique and universal identification for this plant species.

intro: Diplotaxis setosa, also known as the red orache, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its bright red or reddish-purple flowers.
